title: Chemistry: Stoichiometry
description: Mole calculations, balancing equations, and unit conversions in basic stoichiometry.

questions.prompt: How many moles are in $36\text{ g}$ of water ($M = 18\text{ g/mol}$)?

questions.explanation: Coefficients 2, 1, 2 balance hydrogen (4) and oxygen (2) on both sides.

questions.prompt: How many molecules are in $1$ mole of $\text{CO}_2$? (Avogadro's number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation: By definition, 1 mole of any substance contains exactly $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ particles.
